card.desc: > Robert has no systematic way to identify skill gaps across his 30 consultants, making training budget decisions guesswork. This story gives him an exportable report mapping project requirements against internal skills.
card.name: As Robert, I want skill gap reports so I can plan the training budget

card.desc: David was hired with a promise of mentoring that has never materialized because senior consultants are too busy. This story ensures he gets at least one growth assignment per quarter alongside a qualified mentor.
card.name: As David, I want to be matched to growth assignments with a senior mentor so I can develop my skills

card.desc: > Keisha has not taken a vacation in 3 years due to being the most senior consultant and being assigned to almost every project. This story protects her by blocking new assignments when she exceeds 85% allocation.
card.name: As Keisha, I want the system to flag my over-allocation before I get assigned to a new project

card.desc: Staffing a project currently takes Michelle several days of cross-referencing calendars, spreadsheets, and personal knowledge. This story reduces that to under 10 minutes by generating data-driven team suggestions automatically.
card.name: As Michelle, I want optimal team composition suggestions based on skills and availability so I can allocate in under 10 minutes

card.desc: Michelle maintains a personal spreadsheet known as the "black book" to track all consultant allocations. It is a single point of failure — when she is unavailable, the entire allocation process stalls. This story replaces that spreadsheet with a shared system.
card.name: As Michelle, I want a unified view of all allocations so I don't need my personal spreadsheet

card.desc: > Robert currently takes 2-3 days to answer whether SilverTrust can take a new project, relying on gut feeling and manual spreadsheet digging rather than real data. This story addresses that directly by giving him a live dashboard.
card.name: As Robert, I want real-time capacity data so I can answer 'can we take this project?' in under 30 minutes
New resource request
As Michelle, I want optimal team composition suggestions based on skills and availability so I can allocate in under 10 minutes

old.desc:
- Given a new project's skill requirements and hours are entered into Jira, when the system processes them, then it generates at least 3 team composition options within 2 minutes.
- Given the system generates suggestions, then each option must include: a primary recommendation (best skill match), an alternative with a junior + mentor pairing, and a growth opportunity option.
- Given a suggestion is presented, then it must show the skill match percentage (target: >95%), each team member's current workload, and whether any consultant would exceed 85% allocation.
- Given a manager reviews the suggestions, when they approve one, then the allocation is confirmed and relevant calendars/Jira tasks are set up automatically.
- Given a manager disagrees with a suggestion, then they can override it and must provide a brief written justification for reporting purposes.
- Given part-time or fractional allocations are needed, then the system correctly calculates and displays each person's partial contribution (not just headcount).

card.name: As Michelle, I want optimal team composition suggestions based on skills and availability so I can allocate in under 10 minutes
As Michelle, I want a unified view of all allocations so I don't need my personal spreadsheet

old.desc:
- Given a manager opens the unified allocation view, when the page loads, then all active projects and their assigned consultants are visible in a single screen without switching between tools.
- Given a consultant's assignment is updated in the system, when the change is saved, then the unified view reflects it automatically without any manual refresh or spreadsheet update.
- Given a manager previously maintained a personal spreadsheet, when they access the unified view, then it contains at minimum: consultant name, project name, allocation %, start/end dates, and seniority level.
- Given 15–20 active projects exist simultaneously, then the view must load and display all of them without pagination delays.
- Given a manager filters by consultant or project, then results update within 3 seconds.

card.name: As Michelle, I want a unified view of all allocations so I don't need my personal spreadsheet
As Robert, I want real-time capacity data so I can answer 'can we take this project?' in under 30 minutes

old.desc:
- Given a manager opens the capacity dashboard, when the page loads, then all 30 consultants' current availability is displayed within 10 seconds.
- Given a new project is being evaluated, when a manager asks "can we take this?", then the system provides a clear answer (yes/no + available headcount) in under 30 minutes without manual spreadsheet lookup.
- Given capacity data changes (new assignment or project end), when the change is saved, then the dashboard reflects updated availability within 60 seconds.
- Given a consultant is allocated between 80–85%, then the dashboard shows them as "optimally utilized"; below 70% shows "under-utilized"; above 85% shows "at risk of overload".
- Given a project duration is entered, when the system projects future capacity, then the forecast is within ±10% of actual need over the planning horizon.
- Given the dashboard is accessed, then no compensation or personal performance rating data is exposed to managers.

card.name: As Robert, I want real-time capacity data so I can answer 'can we take this project?' in under 30 minutes
As Robert, I want skill gap reports so I can plan the training budget

old.desc:
- Given the VP of Talent opens the skill gap report, when the report loads, then it shows all skills required by active and upcoming projects mapped against the internal skills inventory of the 30 consultants.
- Given a skill gap is identified (i.e., a required skill is not available in-house or is under-represented), then the report highlights it with: skill name, number of projects requiring it, and number of consultants currently holding it.
- Given the report is used for training budget planning, then it must be exportable (CSV or PDF) with the gap summary.
- Given the target is 90% of project skill requirements available internally, then the report shows the current coverage percentage and which gaps would need to be closed to reach 90%.
- Given the report is refreshed, then it must reflect the current state of all consultant skill profiles and active project requirements without manual data entry.
- Given compensation and performance review data exists in the HR database, then it must NOT appear in the skill gap report.

card.name: As Robert, I want skill gap reports so I can plan the training budget
As Keisha, I want the system to flag my over-allocation before I get assigned to a new project

old.desc:
- Given a consultant's combined project allocations exceed 85%, when a new assignment is attempted, then the system blocks the assignment and displays a clear over-allocation warning before confirming.
- Given the system flags an over-allocation, then the warning must include: current allocation %, projects contributing to it, and remaining headroom (e.g., "Keisha is at 90% — 0% available").
- Given Keisha's allocation is at or above 85%, when a manager tries to assign her to a new project, then Keisha receives a Slack or email notification about the attempted assignment and the conflict.
- Given an over-allocation flag is raised, when a manager still wants to proceed, then they must provide a written justification before the system allows the override.
- Given Keisha's allocation drops below 85%, when a new assignment is created, then no over-allocation warning is triggered.
- Given part-time/fractional allocations exist, when totals are summed, then the system calculates the combined percentage correctly across all active projects.

card.name: As Keisha, I want the system to flag my over-allocation before I get assigned to a new project
As David, I want to be matched to growth assignments with a senior mentor so I can develop my skills

old.desc:
- Given David has a declared development goal in his profile, when a new project is being staffed, then the system includes at least one "growth opportunity" team option that assigns David alongside a senior consultant with the relevant skill.
- Given a growth pairing is suggested, then the senior consultant assigned as mentor must not exceed 85% total allocation after the new project is added.
- Given David is assigned to a growth project, then his profile must be updated to reflect the in-progress skill being developed.
- Given a junior consultant completes a growth assignment, when the project ends, then the system prompts the mentor to confirm whether the skill was acquired, and updates the consultant's skill profile accordingly.
- Given the success metric requires each junior consultant gets at least 1 growth assignment per quarter, then the system flags any junior consultant who has not had a growth opportunity in the last 90 days.
- Given David can view his own development goals, when he logs in, then he can see his current growth assignments, completed skill badges, and recommended next opportunities.
